channel.title: Argocd on Anatole HAGBE | DevOps & Cloud Engineer
channel.description: Recent content in Argocd on Anatole HAGBE | DevOps & Cloud Engineer

item.title: GitOps Is Not Just ArgoCD
item.author: ahagbes89@gmail.com (Anatole HAGBE)
item.description:
GitOps has become one of the most overloaded terms in the DevOps space. You mention it in a meeting and everyone nods — but ask them to define it and you’ll get five different answers.

Let me offer a cleaner mental model.

GitOps is a set of principles, not a tool

The OpenGitOps project defines four core principles:

  1. Declarative — The desired state of the system is expressed declaratively
  2. Versioned and immutable — The desired state is stored in a way that preserves history
  3. Pulled automatically — Approved changes are applied automatically
  4. Continuously reconciled — Software agents continuously ensure actual state matches desired state

Notice that none of these say “use ArgoCD” or “use Flux.”

item.title: K8s GitOps Platform
item.author: ahagbes89@gmail.com (Anatole HAGBE)
item.description:
A fully automated GitOps platform built for a team of 30+ engineers. Manages 15 microservices across dev, staging, and production environments.

What it does

  • Declarative application delivery via ArgoCD with automatic sync
  • Helm chart library for standardized service deployments
  • Terraform modules for EKS cluster provisioning and networking
  • Slack notifications for deployment events and failures
  • Automated rollback on failed health checks

Tech Stack

AWS EKS ArgoCD Helm Terraform GitHub Actions Prometheus Grafana
